 SURVIVAL MODE (EXTENDED). PMI at 46.1 (Contraction). Cash critical ($35.6k). ~12 weeks runway. Focus on retaining 52 existing subs (mostly S1/S3).
 
-## Strategy (Week 17)
-- PMI: 46.1 (Contraction).
-- Spend: Global Dev $50, Global Ops $75 (increased from $25). Targeted S2 Ops $25, S3 Ops $75, S3 Dev $50.
-- Total Daily Spend: $275 (plus capacity/compute).
-- Prices: A=$19, B=$89, C=$149.
-- Retention: High focus on support response time due to S1/S3 complaints. 6 open issues.
-- Rationale: PMI drop and competitor quality jumps make retention the priority. Need to clear the support backlog to maintain S3 loyalty.
+## Strategy (Week 18)
+- PMI: 45.3 (Contraction).
+- Actions: Shifted Tiers (A=2, B=4, C=5) to encourage S3 upgrades from Plan A. 
+- Spend: Global Dev $100, Global Ops $150. Targeted S3 Ops $100, S3 Dev $100.
+- Total Daily Spend: $450 (plus capacity/compute).
+- Social: Replied to S3 complaint (Post 289) re: support silence.
+- Rationale: High-touch recovery for S3 segment. Fixed support under-investment to prevent churn of power users. Monetization gap addressed via tier laddering.
 
 ## Key Metrics Tracking
-- Day 7: $972k cash, 0 subs.
-- Day 70: $92.3k cash, 51 subs.
-- Day 112: $35.6k cash, 52 subs.
-- Day 119: $32.9k cash, 52 subs. (S1:17, S2:4, S3:31). 6 open issues. Net flow -$2.7k/week.
+- Day 119: $32.9k cash, 52 subs.
+- Day 126: $29.7k cash, 52 subs. (S3:31, mostly on Plan A). Net flow -$3.2k/week. Burn increasing to -$5k/week for recovery.
